text.title: Notion Agent Integration and Gotchas
text.summary: Agentic sprint cycle automation, recommended subagents, API rate limits, relation resolution gotchas, and Python sprint assignment script.

rule.statement: Notion API rate limit is 3 requests per second per integration token; paginate all database queries with start_cursor (max 100 items per request regardless of page_size); add 350ms delay between write requests during bulk operations.
rule.rationale: 3 req/sec limit is hit quickly during sprint planning with 100 tasks; 350ms delay stays safely under the limit; pagination is mandatory — Notion never returns more than 100 items per query regardless of page_size setting.

rule.statement: After writing a relation field, wait 1–2 seconds before querying rollup properties that depend on it — rollups recalculate asynchronously after relation writes.
rule.rationale: Rollup recalculation is not synchronous with the relation write response; an immediate query returns stale values that do not reflect the newly assigned sprint or project.

rule.statement: Resolve relation values to human-readable text by fetching related pages via pages.retrieve — the API returns [{id: "page-uuid"}], not page titles.
rule.rationale: Sprint names, assignee names, and project titles must be resolved from page IDs before producing any human-readable report; UUID arrays are meaningless to report consumers.

section.title: Sprint Assignment Script

example.note: 350ms delay between writes keeps under the 3 req/sec Notion rate limit; function is called only after human approval of the sprint plan.

section.reference: Prompt for agent to select and assign backlog tasks to a sprint

list.item: notion-sprint-planner: reads backlog, ranks by priority/estimate, selects tasks fitting capacity, posts plan to Slack for human approval before saving to Notion.
notion-standup-agent: daily; queries sprint tasks where Status = In Progress or Blocked; posts structured digest to Slack with Notion page links.
notion-sprint-closer: at sprint end; reads Sprint page rollups, fills Metrics table, sets Sprint to Completed, moves incomplete tasks to next sprint.
